name: SketchupExtension
summary: The SketchupExtension class contains methods allowing you to create and manipulate SketchUp extensions. Extensions are Ruby scripts that can be loaded and unloaded using the Extension manager (Extensions panel of the Extension Manager dialog box). Generally you should register your ruby scripts as an extension to give SketchUp users the ability to disable it through the user interface. The idea here is to take the ruby script that actually creates your functionality and place it in a folder somewhere outside of the /Plugins folder, most commonly a subdirectory like /Plugins/MyExtension. Then you create a new ruby script inside the /Plugins directory that will set up the extension entry and load your original script if the user has your extension turned on. Here is an example extension loading script. For this example, the following code would be saved in /Plugins/StairTools.rb, and the actual plugin itself would live in /Plugins/StairTools/core.rb. You can find two example extensions that ship with SketchUp, su_dynamiccomponents.rb and su_sandboxtools.rb, under the /Plugins/ folder.

constructors.name: initialize
constructors.summary: Note: It is recommended to omit the file extension provided in the path argument. SketchUp will resolve the file extension to .rbe, .rbs or .rb. The new method is used to create a new SketchupExtension object. Note that once the extension object is created, it will not appear in the Extension Manager dialog until your register it with the Sketchup.register_extension method.
constructors.remarks: Note: It is recommended to omit the file extension provided in the path argument. SketchUp will resolve the file extension to .rbe, .rbs or .rb. The new method is used to create a new SketchupExtension object. Note that once the extension object is created, it will not appear in the Extension Manager dialog until your register it with the Sketchup.register_extension method.

params.name: path
params.doc: The relative path to the script that loads your plugin.

methods.name: check
methods.summary: Loads the extension, meaning the underlying ruby script is immediately interpreted.
methods.remarks: Loads the extension, meaning the underlying ruby script is immediately interpreted. This is the equivalent of checking the extension's checkbox in the Extension Manager.

methods.name: extension_path
methods.summary: The extension_path method returns the file system path to the extension's outer rb file.
methods.remarks: The extension_path method returns the file system path to the extension's outer rb file.

returns.doc: the file system path to the extension

methods.name: id
methods.summary: The id method returns the Extension Warehouse ID string.
methods.remarks: The id method returns the Extension Warehouse ID string.

methods.name: load_on_start?
methods.summary: Returns whether the extension is set to load when SketchUp starts up.
methods.remarks: Returns whether the extension is set to load when SketchUp starts up.

methods.name: loaded?
methods.summary: Returns whether the extension is currently loaded, meaning the actual ruby script that implements the extension has been evaluated.
methods.remarks: Returns whether the extension is currently loaded, meaning the actual ruby script that implements the extension has been evaluated.

methods.name: registered?
methods.summary: Returns whether the extension has been registered via Sketchup.
methods.remarks: Returns whether the extension has been registered via Sketchup.register_extension.

methods.name: uncheck
methods.remarks: Unloads the extension. This is the equivalent of unchecking the extension's checkbox in the Extension Manager > Extensions list. Note that technically the extension is not “unloaded” in the sense that it stops running during the current SketchUp session, but the next time the user restarts SketchUp, the extension will not be active.

methods.name: version_id
methods.summary: The version_id method returns the Extension Warehouse Version ID string.
methods.remarks: The version_id method returns the Extension Warehouse Version ID string.

returns.doc: the Extension Warehouse Version ID string

properties.name: copyright
properties.summary: The copyright method returns the copyright string which appears beneath an extension inside the Extensions Manager dialog.
properties.remarks: The copyright method returns the copyright string which appears beneath an extension inside the Extensions Manager dialog.

properties.name: creator
properties.summary: The creator method returns the creator string which appears beneath an extension inside the Extensions Manager dialog.
properties.remarks: The creator method returns the creator string which appears beneath an extension inside the Extensions Manager dialog.

properties.name: description
properties.summary: The description method returns the long description which appears beneath an extension inside the Extensions Manager dialog.
properties.remarks: The description method returns the long description which appears beneath an extension inside the Extensions Manager dialog.

properties.name: name
properties.summary: The name method returns the name which appears for an extension inside the Extensions Manager dialog.
properties.remarks: The name method returns the name which appears for an extension inside the Extensions Manager dialog.

properties.name: version
properties.summary: The version method returns the version which appears beneath an extension inside the Extensions Manager dialog.
properties.remarks: The version method returns the version which appears beneath an extension inside the Extensions Manager dialog.
